title: SARM Capabilities
description: Capability Exchange message per §4.7. POST /sarm/v1/capabilities establishes how the two parties will interact. Schema URI: urn:ietf:params:sarm:api:messages:1.0:Capabilities

role.description: Which party this capability declaration describes.

returnChannel.description: Where the Source System may deliver events and async completions. Presence signals async capability. Absence means synchronous-only; the Source System MUST NOT return 202 per §4.7.

conformanceLevel.description: Source System conformance level per §4.6. Only present in sourceSystem responses.

supportsAsync.description: Whether the Source System processes decisions asynchronously (returns 202). Only present in sourceSystem responses.

supportsConditionalGet.description: Whether the Source System honors If-None-Match conditional requests per §4.5. Only present in sourceSystem responses.

events.description: Event types this Source System will emit. Source-System-determined strings declared through the capability exchange. A consumer MUST ignore types it does not recognize per §7.2.

signing.description: Message signing configuration per §11.6 and §4.7. Bidirectional: Source System signs ScopeItems and events; Attestation Engine signs Decisions.

signs.description: Whether this party signs the messages it sends.

requiresPeerSignatures.description: Whether this party requires the peer to sign messages.

algorithms.description: Signing algorithm(s) this party uses (e.g., ES256).

key.description: Public key, carried inline or referenced. See §11.6.

description: The set of capability fields is illustrative and extensible; a party MUST ignore capability fields it does not recognize per §4.7.
